CheckTitle: Default Global Session Policy applies a Network Zone condition aligned with the Access Control Policy
Description: Every active Global Session Policy must use the **IF User's IP is** condition, mapped to a Network Zone, on its **Priority 1 active rule** (DISA STIG V-279691 / OKTA-APP-003242). Okta evaluates rules in priority order, and policies by group assignment, so the zone condition must sit on the rule that fires first for each policy that governs users.
Risk: When the Global Session Policy does not restrict access by Network Zone, every authenticated entity is granted session establishment regardless of source IP. Authorization that does not factor in network location lets stolen credentials or out-of-band sessions reach the Okta dashboard from any internet-routable address, undermining the organization's Access Control Policy.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the Okta Admin Console as a Super Admin
2. Go to Security > Networks and define the Network Zones (allow / deny) that match the organization's Access Control Policy
3. Go to Security > Global Session Policy
4. Open the Default Policy
5. Add or edit a non-default rule
6. In Conditions, set 'IF User's IP is' to In zone (allow) or Not in zone (deny) and select the Network Zone
7. Save the rule
Code.Terraform:
resource "okta_policy_rule_signon" "prowler_network_zone" {
  policy_id        = okta_policy_signon.default.id
  name             = "Prowler-enforced Network Zone"
  status           = "ACTIVE"
  network_connection = "ZONE"
  network_includes  = [okta_network_zone.allowed.id]
}

Recommendation.Text: Add or update a non-default rule in the Default Global Session Policy so it maps the IF User's IP condition to a Network Zone that reflects the organization's Access Control Policy.
